Medical.question: When you imagine yourself working in healthcare 10 years from now, where do you picture yourself?

options.label: A
options.text: In a hospital treating patients directly

options.label: B
options.text: In a research lab discovering cures

options.label: C
options.text: In a community helping large groups stay healthy

options.label: D
options.text: Running a pharmacy or health business

Medical.trait_map: Patient care / Research / Public health / Entrepreneurship

Medical.question: A patient comes in with an unknown condition. What excites you most?

options.label: A
options.text: Diagnosing and treating them immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Running tests and analysing results

options.label: C
options.text: Understanding how their lifestyle caused it

options.label: D
options.text: Finding which medicine works best

Medical.question: How do you feel about long years of study (5–8 years) before you earn well?

options.label: A
options.text: Totally fine — I am committed to medicine fully

options.label: B
options.text: Okay if I can specialise in something I love

options.label: C
options.text: I prefer a 3-yr course and start working sooner

options.label: D
options.text: I want to study and earn simultaneously

Medical.question: Which subject in biology class did you enjoy most?

options.label: A
options.text: Human physiology and anatomy

options.label: B
options.text: Genetics and cell biology

options.label: C
options.text: Ecology and environment

options.label: D
options.text: Chemistry of medicines and drugs

Medical.question: How comfortable are you with physical contact with patients (injections, examinations)?

options.label: A
options.text: Very comfortable — I want direct patient contact daily

options.label: B
options.text: Somewhat — I prefer minimal contact

options.label: C
options.text: I prefer working with machines and equipment

options.label: D
options.text: I prefer working with data and reports

Medical.question: What kind of work environment suits you best?

options.label: A
options.text: Fast-paced emergency or OT setting

options.label: B
options.text: Quiet lab or research setting

options.label: C
options.text: Community / outdoor fieldwork

options.label: D
options.text: Office or hospital admin setting

Medical.question: If you had to choose only one, what matters most to you in a career?

options.label: A
options.text: Saving individual lives directly

options.label: B
options.text: Discovering something new for humanity

options.label: C
options.text: Improving health of communities and society

options.label: D
options.text: Financial stability and business growth

Medical.trait_map: Clinical doctor / Researcher / Public health / Entrepreneur

Medical.question: How do you feel about working night shifts and long unpredictable hours?

options.label: A
options.text: I accept it fully — it comes with the profession

options.label: B
options.text: I can manage a few years but want balance later

options.label: C
options.text: I strongly prefer fixed day hours

options.label: D
options.text: I prefer to set my own schedule

Medical.question: Which allied field sounds most interesting to you?

options.label: A
options.text: Imaging — X-Ray, MRI, CT scans (MRIT/Radiology)

options.label: B
options.text: Rehab — Physiotherapy or Occupational Therapy

options.label: C
options.text: Lab work — testing blood, tissues, cultures

options.label: D
options.text: Nutrition, diet and preventive wellness

Medical.trait_map: Radiology tech / Physio/OT / Lab tech / Dietitian

Medical.question: What is your family's approximate annual income? (This helps match scholarships)

options.label: A
options.text: Below ₹2.5 lakh

options.label: B
options.text: ₹2.5 lakh – ₹6 lakh

options.label: C
options.text: ₹6 lakh – ₹12 lakh

options.label: D
options.text: Above ₹12 lakh

Non-Medical.question: What would you most enjoy doing on a typical workday?

options.label: A
options.text: Building something physical — machines, circuits, structures

options.label: B
options.text: Writing code and solving software problems

options.label: C
options.text: Designing products, interfaces, or visual systems

options.label: D
options.text: Analysing data, solving complex mathematical problems

Non-Medical.question: When something breaks at home, what is your first instinct?

options.label: A
options.text: Try to fix it yourself by understanding how it works

options.label: B
options.text: Look up solutions and test different approaches

options.label: C
options.text: Think about whether it could be designed better

options.label: D
options.text: Ignore it and call someone who knows

Non-Medical.question: Which project would you most enjoy in school?

options.label: A
options.text: Building a working robot or mechanical model

options.label: B
options.text: Creating an app or a website

options.label: C
options.text: Designing a poster, logo, or 3D model

options.label: D
options.text: Analysing statistics and presenting findings

Non-Medical.question: How do you feel about JEE preparation (1–2 years of intense study)?

options.label: A
options.text: I am already preparing / fully committed to JEE

options.label: B
options.text: I gave it a try but did not qualify / moving on

options.label: C
options.text: I prefer to skip JEE and take a direct B.Sc route

options.label: D
options.text: I want a defence or government path after 12th

Non-Medical.question: If you could work on any of these real-world problems, which excites you most?

options.label: A
options.text: Building bridges, smart cities, or clean energy systems

options.label: B

options.label: C
options.text: Designing cars, aircraft, or consumer products

options.label: D

Non-Medical.question: How do you feel about fieldwork — going to construction sites, factories, or outdoor locations?

options.label: A
options.text: I love it — I want a hands-on, on-the-ground career

options.label: B
options.text: Sometimes fine, but I prefer mostly office/lab work

options.label: C
options.text: I prefer fully indoor, desk-based, or remote work

options.label: D
options.text: I want to serve the country through defence

Non-Medical.question: Which technology area do you find most fascinating?

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: AI, machine learning, and data

options.label: C

options.label: D

Non-Medical.question: How important is salary in the first 2 years of your career?

options.label: A
options.text: Very important — I need to support my family quickly

options.label: B
options.text: Important but I am okay with 1–2 years of low pay

options.label: C
options.text: Not a priority — I will go for higher studies first

options.label: D
options.text: I want a govt job with stable pay from day one

Non-Medical.question: What best describes your relationship with mathematics?

options.label: A
options.text: I genuinely love maths — it is my strongest subject

options.label: B
options.text: I am decent at maths but biology or computing is stronger

options.label: C
options.text: I find applied maths okay but pure theory bores me

options.label: D

options.label: B

options.label: C

options.label: C

options.label: C
